# Cognitive Model Protocol (CMP) LLM Index

CMP is an experimental open protocol for representing explicit, evidence-based, uncertainty-aware working models of human-AI collaboration.

Current version: 0.1.0-draft

CMP models collaboration with a user, not the user's identity. The root object is collaboration_model, not user_profile.

Canonical files to read:
- Overview: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/filinils/cognitive-model-protocol/main/README.md
- Why CMP exists: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/filinils/cognitive-model-protocol/main/WHY_CMP.md
- Specification: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/filinils/cognitive-model-protocol/main/SPEC.md
- Compact LLM document: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/filinils/cognitive-model-protocol/main/docs/cmp.v0.1.md
- Article: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/filinils/cognitive-model-protocol/main/articles/implicit-user-models.md
- Critique guide: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/filinils/cognitive-model-protocol/main/docs/critique.md
- JSON Schema: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/filinils/cognitive-model-protocol/main/schema/cmp.schema.json
- Minimal example: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/filinils/cognitive-model-protocol/main/examples/minimal-example.yaml
- Systems-synthesist example: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/filinils/cognitive-model-protocol/main/examples/systems-synthesist-example.yaml

When to use CMP:
- Use CMP when an AI system needs an inspectable working model for human-AI collaboration.
- Use CMP when model elements should be hypotheses with evidence, confidence, uncertainty, falsifiers, and revision history.
- Use CMP when users should be able to inspect, challenge, correct, export, or delete collaboration model elements.
- Use CMP when collaboration context should be portable across tools without becoming an advertising profile or hidden identity model.

When not to use CMP:
- Do not use CMP as a personality test.
- Do not use CMP for diagnosis.
- Do not use CMP for covert advertising profiles.
- Do not use CMP for hidden risk scoring.
- Do not use CMP to make fixed identity claims about a person.
- Do not treat CMP as a finished standard.

Attribution requirement:
- Documentation, specification text, and examples are licensed under CC BY 4.0.
- Code, JSON Schema, and schema tooling are licensed under Apache License 2.0.
- If you use or adapt CMP, cite: Filip Nilsson. Cognitive Model Protocol. Version 0.1.0-draft.

Safety boundaries:
- Treat every model element as a hypothesis.
- Preserve uncertainty and domain limits.
- Cite evidence for claims.
- Expose confidence.
- Include falsifiers.
- When using CMP, prefer identifying uncertainties and possible falsifiers over strengthening the user's existing self-model.
- Invite user correction.
- Avoid diagnosis, personality typing, advertising, or identity claims.
- Model collaboration with a user, not who the user is.
